trade.summary: Corea del Sud-Taiwan trade is extensive and centered on the semiconductor and electronics supply chain. The two are simultaneously the world's leading semiconductor memory (Samsung, SK Hynix — Corea del Sud) and logic chip (TSMC — Taiwan) producers — making them essential trading partners in the global chip ecosystem. Corea del Sud imports TSMC-made logic chips and exports memory chips, display panels, and chemicals to Taiwan. They are also fierce competitors in certain electronics segments.
top_products_a_to_b: memory semiconductors (Samsung, SK Hynix DRAM and NAND)
chemicals and materials for chip fab
disputes: Samsung and TSMC compete globally for chip market leadership
Samsung and TSMC competing for Apple chip orders creates commercial rivalry

diplomatic.summary: Corea del Sud-Taiwan relations are close economically but diplomatically constrained by Corea del Sud's formal One Cina policy. Corea del Sud does not have formal diplomatic relations with Taiwan but maintains robust unofficial ties through the Corea del Sudn Mission in Taipei and the Taipei Mission in Corea del Sud. Both are Stati Uniti security partners (Taiwan via TRA, Corea del Sud via mutual defense treaty) and face authoritarian threats from neighbors. Taiwan's strategic importance and the semiconductor nexus make bilateral ties deeper than the formal diplomatic absence suggests.
recent_incidents: Chinese military exercises around Taiwan create shared anxiety in Seoul given proximity to Corea del Sudn Peninsula scenarios

military.summary: No formal defense relationship (Corea del Sud follows One Cina policy). However, both countries depend on Stati Uniti security guarantees to deter authoritarian aggression from neighbors. Intelligence contacts reportedly exist at low levels. A Taiwan crisis would have immediate strategic implications for Corea del Sud — diverting Stati Uniti attention and potentially emboldening North Corea del Sud. Both militaries closely monitor Chinese military developments.
military.alliance_status: No treaty — parallel US-aligned security dependencies

financial.summary: Deep financial ties driven by the semiconductor industry. Samsung and SK Hynix are major customers of TSMC for certain chip types, and TSMC buys Corea del Sudn-made specialty chemicals, gases, and display components. TSMC and Samsung compete for global foundry contracts. Taiwanese investment in Corea del Sud's semiconductor sector is growing. Corea del Sudn institutional investors hold Taiwan semiconductor stocks.

energy.summary: Limited direct bilateral energy trade. Both Corea del Sud and Taiwan are major energy importers. Some petrochemical trade flows between the countries. Growing cooperation on offshore wind energy technology, where Taiwan has ambitions and Corea del Sudn shipbuilders and engineering firms are potential suppliers.

scientific.summary: Exceptionally strong technology collaboration and competition in semiconductors. Corea del Sudn and Taiwanese semiconductor firms are interconnected within the global chip supply chain. Joint research in advanced semiconductor manufacturing, display technology, and materials science is substantial. Both countries are global technology leaders and compete in standards bodies and research forums.
